messages.content: judy moody and the not bummer summer USA DVD Releases date

"Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer" is a family comedy film based on the Judy Moody series by Megan McDonald. The movie was released in theaters in the United States on June 10, 2011. The DVD release for the film in the USA occurred later in the same year. Although I do not have the exact date post my knowledge cutoff, it is common for DVDs to be released a few months after the theatrical release. Generally, you can expect a typical window of about 3-4 months for a DVD to come out following its cinema run, so a late 2011 date would have been likely for "Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer." For the exact date, it's best to check a reliable DVD release database or the film's official website for the announcement details.
judy moody and the not bummer summer USA DVD is a production of

"Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer" is produced by Smokewood Entertainment Group. The film was developed in collaboration with the creators of the Judy Moody book series, with Megan McDonald (the author of the books) involved in the writing process for the screenplay. The movie was directed by John Schultz and starred Jordana Beatty as Judy Moody. 

As for distribution, which includes the release of the DVD, the film was distributed by Relativity Media in the United States. Therefore, the DVD would also likely have been distributed through Relativity Media or their home entertainment distribution partners at the time of the release.

The DVD release of "Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer" would have been a product of the film's original production company in partnership with the distributor responsible for home media. The production company for the film is Smokewood Entertainment Group, while the distribution would have been handled by Relativity Media, which was responsible for the theatrical release in the United States.

When DVDs are produced, they are typically manufactured and distributed by the studio or a distribution partner that specializes in home entertainment. The distributor oversees creating the physical DVD, which includes the pressing, packaging, and distributing the DVDs to retailers, rental stores, and online vendors.

So, if you're looking at a DVD copy of "Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer," it is a product of Smokewood Entertainment Group's creative work, but the physical production and distribution of the DVD would have involved Relativity Media or their home entertainment distribution partners.

As with all Disney films, the DVD was distributed by Walt Disney Studios Home Entertainment, which handles the production and distribution of Disney's home entertainment media.

As of my knowledge cutoff date in March 2023, there is no production titled "Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer Meet PAW Patrol." "Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer" is a standalone film based on Megan McDonald's "Judy Moody" book series, and its production is not directly related to "PAW Patrol," which is an entirely separate animated television series produced by Spin Master Entertainment, in association with Nickelodeon and Guru Studio.

"Judy Moody and the NOT Bummer Summer" is a production of Smokewood Entertainment Group and was distributed by Relativity Media.

"PAW Patrol" is an animated series aimed at children that follows the adventures of a group of rescue dogs who work together to protect their community. If any collaborations or crossover productions between the two entities were announced after my knowledge cutoff or if any special content was released, it would be best to look for the most current and updated information through official press releases or entertainment news outlets.

"The Nut Job" is an animated comedy film that was released in theaters in 2014. The production companies involved in the creation of "The Nut Job" are Open Road Films, ToonBox Entertainment, Redrover Co., Ltd., and Gulfstream Pictures. The film was distributed theatrically in the United States by Open Road Films.

When it comes to the DVD release of "The Nut Job" in the USA, the physical disc production and distribution would typically be managed by the home entertainment division of the theatrical distributor or a home media distribution partner. In this case, the DVD release would have been handled by Universal Pictures Home Entertainment, as they were the distributor for the home media format.

To identify the exact production company or distributor of "The Nut Job" DVD in New Zealand, it's best to look at the DVD packaging or an official local retailer's or distributor's detailed listing. Die Hard USA Releases date

"Die Hard" is an iconic action film starring Bruce Willis that was originally released in theaters in the United States on July 15, 1988.

If you are asking about the release date for "Die Hard" on home video (VHS, DVD, Blu-ray) in the USA, the original home video releases would have been several months after the theatrical debut, which was the common practice at the time. However, exact release dates for historical home video formats like VHS are harder to pinpoint.

For more contemporary home media formats, "Die Hard" has had various DVD and Blu-ray releases over the years, including special editions, anniversary editions, and box sets containing the entire film series. Each of these would have its own specific release date. If you're looking for a specific edition or format, additional details would be needed to provide an exact release date.
